title: Qyl Telemetry Capability Graph (TCG)
description: A complete, machine-readable, provenance-tagged manifest of every telemetry capability a qyl-instrumented binary can produce. Generated deterministically from the instrumentation contract — never sampled. Forward compatibility is handled by version-matched validation and tolerant consumer code, NOT by loosening this schema: the enums below are intentionally closed, so a document is validated strictly against the schema whose version equals its own 'schemaVersion'. A consumer reading a newer document validates it against that newer schema; in code it maps any unrecognized enum member to the documented catch-all and ignores unknown object properties (additionalProperties stays true).

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of THIS document format. Within a major version, changes are additive; consumers tolerate unknown fields. The '-draft' pre-release marks a format that may still change.

generator.description: Fully-qualified type that produced this document (provenance of the manifest itself).

capabilityCount.description: Number of entries in 'capabilities'. MUST equal capabilities.length.

signals.description: Per-signal capability counts (a fast summary so a consumer can size dashboards before reading the full list).

capabilities.description: Every telemetry capability the binary can produce, in deterministic contract order.

id.description: Stable contract identifier (e.g. OTEL_DOTNET_AUTO_CONTRACT_001). Use this as the join key across TCG versions.

signal.description: OpenTelemetry signal this capability contributes to.

lane.description: The qyl mechanism lane that owns this capability.

status.description: Implementation status of the capability in the current build.

payloadAccess.description: How the lane accesses the underlying payload: typed-public (AOT-safe), reflection-required (not AOT-safe), or not-applicable (compile-time only).

provenance.description: The load-bearing distinction: whether the capability's attribute keys are owned at compile time, carried as runtime payload values, are a control surface (env/option), or unsupported on the AOT substrate. Consumers MUST map unknown members to 'unknown'.

supportedVersions.description: Human-readable supported version range(s) of the instrumented library, or '*'.

owner.description: The component that produces this capability (a generator, the public telemetry listeners, etc.).

attributes.description: Declared semantic-convention attribute keys this capability sets. Empty until a lane populates the contract's AttributeNames (e.g. the future DTO-inference lane).
